label: Injil vs. New Testament: Key Differences

source.title: What Muslims Mean by 'Injil' and Why It Matters
source.summary: The Qur'an refers to the 'Injil' (Gospel) as revelation given to Jesus (Isa). However, what the Qur'an means and what Christians mean often differ: ISLAMIC VIEW: The Injil was a single book revealed directly to Jesus, similar to how the Qur'an was revealed to Muhammad. Many Muslims believe this original Injil was lost or corrupted, and the current New Testament is not the true Injil. CHRISTIAN VIEW: The 'Gospel' (Greek: euangelion) is the good news about Jesus, recorded by multiple witnesses in four Gospel accounts (Matthew, Mark, Luke, John), plus letters and Acts. This is not a book given TO Jesus but testimony ABOUT Jesus. The New Testament comprises 27 books written by eyewitnesses and apostles. BRIDGE POINT: When discussing scripture, clarify what each person means. Christians can affirm the 'good news' (Injil) about Jesus IS preserved—in the New Testament documents that testify to His life, death, and resurrection. Understanding this linguistic and conceptual gap prevents talking past each other.
